ui-ux-pro-max.summary: Design intelligence for web/mobile UI. Always run --design-system before implementing visual work.
when_to_use: New page, dashboard, landing, admin panel, or mobile screen
Visual refactor, accessibility review, or design-system tokens
Choosing colors, typography, layout, or interaction patterns
skip_when: Pure backend, API, database, or DevOps-only tasks
Performance work unrelated to the interface
ui-ux-pro-max.graph_hint: Preflight design-system -> (optional) stack/domain search -> Implement UI. Read SKILL.md + preflight output.

workflow.title: Analyze prompt
workflow.action: Extract product type, audience, style keywords, and project stack (react, nextjs, shadcn, vue, etc.).

workflow.title: Preflight: design system (REQUIRED)
workflow.action: Parent runs CLI; attach full stdout to the implementation subagent prompt.

workflow.title: Supplement (as needed)
workflow.action: Run domain or stack searches before coding if the task needs extra detail (ux, chart, shadcn, etc.).

workflow.title: Implement UI
workflow.action: Follow tokens from design-system output; use Quick Reference checklist in SKILL.md before delivery.

preflight.label: Generate design system
preflight.when: Any task that changes how the UI looks, feels, or behaves
preflight.notes: Windows: py -3. Unix: replace command with python3 and drop -3. Attach output to subagent prompt.

preflight.label: Persist design system to project
preflight.when: Multi-session work; team shares tokens via git
preflight.notes: Creates design-system/MASTER.md and optional design-system/pages/<page>.md. Run from project root.

grilling.summary: Structured interview — one question at a time with recommendations.
grilling.graph_hint: Add an Interview node before implementation; wait for user answer each turn.

canvas.summary: Rich visual artifact for data-heavy or analytical deliverables.
canvas.graph_hint: Prefer canvas over markdown tables when the deliverable is exploratory.

_example.summary: Template entry — copy to skill-runbooks.user.json or use `npx maestro-skills runbook add`.
_example.graph_hint: Optional node title for the dependency graph.
